text: The Bihar Combined Entrance Competitive Examination Board (BCECEB) conducts the BCECE (Lateral Entry) exam for diploma holders who wish to join the second year of B.E./B.Tech programs in engineering colleges across Bihar. This pathway allows students with a 3-year Polytechnic/Diploma to skip the first year of engineering, making it a

quicker route to obtaining a B.Tech degree certificate. Educational Qualification: Candidates must have completed a diploma in the relevant branch of engineering/technology with at least 45% marks (40% for reserved categories sc and st). =>If you are last year in the diploma then eligible for applications. =>No Age Limit =>No

Branch Restrictions Online Registration: Candidates need to register on the official BCECEB website. Application Form: Fill out the application form with personal, educational, and contact details. Upload Documents: Upload a recent photograph and signature. Application Fee: The application fee is ₹2200 for all categories Submission: Submit the application form and

take a printout for counselling. official website visit BCECE LE portal Grammar and Comprehension: Subject-Verb Concord Tense Voice Synthesis Common Errors Vocabulary Unseen Passage Business Correspondence: Drafting Official and Business Letters Drafting Circulars and Official Orders/Instructions Drafting Minutes and Agenda of Meetings Elementary Spoken English: Elementary Theory of Phonetics Sentence

and Paragraph Writing: Constructing Sentences and Paragraphs Report Writing: Importance of Reports in the Industrial Setup Classification of Reports Writing Reports: Format and Mechanics of Language Technical Description of Things and Processes: Describing Technical Items and Processes Precis Writing: Summarizing Texts Solid Geometry: Cartesian, cylindrical, polar, and spherical polar coordinates

Equations of planes and straight lines Coordinate transformations Spheres, cones, cylinders, ellipsoids, paraboloids, and hyperboloids Differential Calculus: Limits, continuity, and differentiability Applications of differentiation Successive differentiation Taylor and Maclaurin expansions Integral Calculus: Definite and indefinite integrals Applications of integration Beta and Gamma functions Multiple integrals Differential Equations: First-order and second-order

differential equations Methods of solution Systems of first-order equations Vector Calculus: Scalar and vector fields Gradient, divergence, and curl Line and surface integrals Theorems of Green, Gauss, and Stokes Matrices: Algebra of matrices Determinants and inverse of a matrix Eigenvalues and eigenvectors Solutions of linear equations Fourier Series: Convergence of

Fourier series Dirichlet conditions Half-range series Mechanics Introduction: Idealization of mechanics Concept of rigid body External forces and laws of mechanics Vector Methods: Equality and equivalence of vectors Principle of transmissibility of forces Moment of force and couple Various Systems of Forces: Statically equivalent force systems Simple equivalent of a

system of forces Equilibrium: Force analysis Free body diagrams Equations of equilibrium Friction: Static, kinetic, and rolling friction Applications to inclined planes, wedges, and pulleys Kinematics and Kinetics of a Particle: Rectilinear and curvilinear motion Projectile motion Impulse and Momentum: Linear impulse and momentum Central impact of elastic, semi-elastic, and

plastic bodies Kinematics and Kinetics of Rigid Bodies: Angular velocity and acceleration D’Alembert’s principle Plane motion of rigid bodies Work, Energy, and Power: Work done by forces and couples Potential, elastic, and kinetic energy Conservation of energy =>Each question consists of 4 marks and compulsory to pass in English qualifying

marks is 35 marks.
